# function: jmespath_query

Evaluates a [JMESPath](https://jmespath.org/) expression against a Terraform value and returns the matching result. Useful for extracting fields from large nested structures (decoded API responses, manifests, configuration trees) without long chains of `try(local.x.foo[0].bar, null)`.

The expression follows the JMESPath specification — projections (`[*]`), filters (`[?key == 'value']`), pipes (`|`), functions (`length`, `sort_by`, `to_string`, …), and multi-select hashes (`{a: foo, b: bar}`) are all supported. Returns `null` when the expression matches nothing.

Backed by [jmespath-community/go-jmespath](https://github.com/jmespath-community/go-jmespath), the actively-maintained fork of the canonical Go implementation.

## Example Usage

```terraform
// JMESPath query — extract fields from a nested structure without long try() chains.
output "active_users" {
  value = provider::burnham::jmespath_query(
    {
      users = [
        { name = "alice", active = true },
        { name = "bob", active = false },
        { name = "carol", active = true },
      ]
    },
    "users[?active].name",
  )
  // → ["alice", "carol"]
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
jmespath_query(value dynamic, expression string) dynamic
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `value` (Dynamic) The value to query.
1. `expression` (String) A JMESPath expression.
